name: Louisiana Banana Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 cups sifted Swans Down cake flour
1/2 cup shortening (use butter or margarine, with sour milk or buttermilk)
1 cup mashed ripe bananas (3 or 4 bananas)
recipeInstructions:
Mix sour milk or buttermilk with butter or margarine (use 1/2 cup minus 2 tablespoons sour milk or buttermilk). Use vegetable or any other shortening for the remaining amount.
Grease a 13 x 9-inch pan. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
Measure cake flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar into a sifter. Measure shortening into a mixing bowl. Stir shortening just to soften. Stir in dry ingredients; add milk and mashed bananas, then beat until all flour is dampened. Add eggs, nuts, and remaining milk; beat for 2 minutes on low speed.
Pour batter into pan. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes.
Coconut Topping:
Cream butter or margarine with brown sugar. Stir in milk until smooth. Add coconut. Let cake stand in pan for 5 minutes. Slice bananas diagonally into 1/4-inch slices and arrange them in four rows on the cake. Spread hot cake evenly with coconut topping. Place under broiler for 3 minutes, or until sugar is bubbly and coconut is brown. Serve warm.
